anti_patterns.title: AI-purple/pink gradient substitutes for a product story
anti_patterns.do_not: Use a familiar purple-to-pink hero gradient as a shortcut for a product-specific hook, mechanism, or proof object.

anti_patterns.title: System-font-only typography erases the maker voice
anti_patterns.do_not: Ship the authored story with only generic system or commodity sans-serif families instead of a deliberate Korean display and body pairing.

anti_patterns.title: Emoji substitutes for an authored icon system
anti_patterns.do_not: Use emoji as feature, benefit, trust, or purchase icons in place of product-grounded visual evidence or one coherent icon language.

anti_patterns.title: Centered-everything section stack
anti_patterns.do_not: Center every content band; preserve at least one asymmetric product, comparison, or evidence composition that creates hierarchy.

anti_patterns.title: Mobile sticky purchase CTA explicitly disabled
anti_patterns.do_not: Hide the maker-owned sticky purchase action at the mobile breakpoint when the long image-band story pushes the decision controls far off-screen.

anti_patterns.title: Critical purchase terms trapped in raster images
anti_patterns.do_not: Bake price, delivery, compatibility, and return conditions into images that cannot be searched, translated, or updated.

good_bad.good: The detail band is visual, while purchase terms remain semantic text near the decision.
good_bad.bad: Delivery and return terms exist only as pixels in a promotional image.

anti_patterns.title: Low-resolution text baked into long image bands
anti_patterns.do_not: Use unreadable baked-in typography that fails on the narrow mobile authored column.

anti_patterns.title: Repeated discount or delivery claims replace proof
anti_patterns.do_not: Repeat offer language instead of adding mechanism, comparative, or product-use evidence.
